sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, has been experiencing chronic back pain for approximately 2 years, which started after moving furniture one weekend.
The pain is described as a sharp stabbing pain in the back that radiates down to the right leg, including the big toe.
He reports numbness and tingling in the right leg, particularly in the foot, and difficulty getting comfortable, especially when sitting.
He denies any loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area, weakness, loss of bladder or bowel control, or any family members with spine conditions.
He has tried physical therapy and strong pain medications in the past but has not found relief.
Past medical history: The patient quit smoking 20 years ago and has a history of trying various treatments for his back pain.
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in the right leg.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
There is pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The patient has decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has symptoms consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy; chronic pain.
Plan:
I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
The injection will be performed under fluoroscopy, and we will monitor the patient's response to the treatment and schedule follow-up injections as needed, not to exceed four times a year.
Follow-up: We will schedule a follow-up appointment to monitor the patient's response to the injection.
